title: Stale ContractId reference causes unrecoverable runtime abort
source_title: DAML Lifecycle Pattern - Contract Reference Management
description: A Settlement contract stores a raw ContractId to an Obligation. If the Obligation is archived (e.g. cancelled) between the Settlement's creation and execution, the fetch will fail with a runtime error, aborting the entire transaction. The error message provides no context about what went wrong.
recommendation: For contracts that may be independently archived, use contract key lookups (lookupByKey/fetchByKey) at execution time instead of storing raw ContractIds. Handle the None/missing case gracefully.

oracle.description: Fetch of stored ContractId should handle the case where the referenced contract has been archived.
oracle.static_signal: Template field of type ContractId used in fetch without preceding existence check; referenced template has consuming choices exercisable by other parties
oracle.symbolic_property: forall s o. field(s, obligationCid) == cid && archived(cid) => not(aborts(Execute, s))

notes: Very common DAML anti-pattern. ContractIds are ephemeral references; contracts may be archived at any time by their signatories.
